input.label: Select the Base URL for the CrowdStrike Instance

section.p: Review the following documents for specific information about this Splunk Technical Add-On and the associated APIs.

div.a: CrowdStrike Falcon Detection Admin and Configuration Guide

li.a: CrowdStrike Falcon Incident, Detection, and Alert Monitoring APIs

section.p: Note: For documentation within the Falcon UI, the correct base URL should be selected from the dropdown at the top of the page.
This technical add-on is powered by the CrowdStrike FalconPy SDK. For more information about the FalconPy SDK please review the following

ul.li: FalconPy GitHub Page: FalconPy Github Page
FalconPy on Reddit: FalconPy on Reddit

section.p: For information about all the CrowdStrike SDKs please review: Falcon-SDKs
Note: For documentation within the Falcon UI, the correct base URL should be selected from the dropdown at the top of the page.
The technical add-on is supported by CrowdStrike for current CrowdStrike customers.

ol.li: Collect the necessary information and log data as described in the TA guide: CrowdStrike Falcon Detections Admin and Configuration Guide
Open a support ticket with the necessary information and log data in the CrowdStrike Support Portal: CrowdStrike Support Portal

section.p: Please Note: CrowdStrike technical support engineers (TSEs) are required to evaluate Splunk integration support requests. In addition, CrowdStrike TSEs are required to perform troubleshooting workflows to help identify potential issues and evaluate those issues for potential escalations to other teams. This may include, but is not limited to, requesting additional information/data/logs and requesting results from specific search queries or configurations modifications. The inability or unwillingness to supply the required/requested information and/or make request modifications/actions may result in CrowdStrike not being able to troubleshoot the reported issue and result in the inability to provide support for the reported issue.

section.a: Cool Query Friday - Search Examples on Reddit
